title: geigeria angolensis subsp. angolensis
intro: Geigeria angolensis subsp. angolensis, a plant species in the family Asteraceae, is commonly known as the bitter bush or bitter shrub. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In terms of herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Geigeria angolensis subsp. angolensis are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are then administered to patients in various forms, including oral consumption and topical application.
